counters.reason: Fiora punishes predictable spell patterns and performs well in extended side-lane states.
counters.winCondition: Parry or dodge Aatrox's key engage windows, then force controlled trades after his cooldowns are spent.
tips: Do not waste Riposte early; save it for the highest-value punish window.
Trade after Aatrox uses his first spell rotation.
Play side lane with vision and avoid unnecessary teamfights if splitpush is your main win condition.

counters.reason: Irelia can break Aatrox spacing with mobility and punish missed skill windows.
counters.winCondition: Use minions for dash angles, avoid bad wave states, and punish every missed Aatrox spell zone.
tips: Keep minions available for mobility resets.
Only commit after Aatrox loses his strongest spell angle.
Do not overforce when the wave is bad.

counters.reason: Camille can punish poor spacing and transition lane control into strong side-lane pressure.
counters.winCondition: Keep trades short, abuse shield timing, and punish Aatrox after he commits too much into the wave.
tips: Trade around passive shield timing.
Use Hookshot as a punish tool, not as random aggression.
Push your lead into side-lane tempo instead of overchasing kills.

counters.reason: Poppy disrupts dash-heavy engage patterns and makes Ambessa much more linear.
counters.winCondition: Play around anti-dash timing, punish overcommitment, and deny clean entry angles.
tips: Hold W for the real commit instead of using it early.
Fight near walls whenever possible.
Do not overpush without vision.

counters.reason: Malphite slows the lane down and forces Ambessa into lower-value fights.
counters.winCondition: Make lane stable, stack armor, and move the game toward coordinated teamfights.
tips: Do not ego-fight without item advantage.
Use your ultimate for high-value engage windows.
Let Ambessa make the first mistake.

counters.reason: Quinn can deny straightforward engages through range, spacing and tempo.
counters.winCondition: Keep distance, punish failed engage attempts and convert lane advantage into map tempo.
tips: Respect all-in timing when your wave is bad.
Do not waste disengage before the real commit.
Roam with your lead instead of staying top forever.

counters.reason: Jax is excellent in extended trades and Counter Strike lowers Camille's engage reliability.
counters.winCondition: Look for longer trades, use E with discipline, and move into side lane once scaling kicks in.
tips: Do not waste Counter Strike too early.
Trade when Camille cannot fully use her shield or Hookshot.
You do not need to stomp lane if your side lane is winning later.

counters.reason: Poppy stops dash patterns and makes Camille far more predictable in lane.
counters.winCondition: Use W reactively, fight near walls, and turn the lane into a controlled, low-variance matchup.
tips: Save Steadfast Presence for Camille's real commit.
Fight near walls for higher punish value.
Do not hand over free roam timers.

counters.reason: Gragas neutralizes Camille with wave control, sustain, and disengage.
counters.winCondition: Keep the lane stable, deny easy all-ins, and get value through safer scaling and utility.
tips: Use Body Slam reactively.
Keep the wave in a neutral state.
Your lane does not need to be flashy to be effective.

counters.reason: Range poke and blind timing make Darius trades far less reliable.
counters.winCondition: Keep the wave on your side, punish pull windows, and respect Ghost all-in timings.
tips: Use blind on his real commit, not randomly.
Play with vision so you do not die to jungle punish.
Do not greed for extra trades when the wave is bad.

counters.reason: Quinn controls spacing and punishes Darius outside his pull window.
counters.winCondition: Abuse early range, avoid bad crashes, and translate lane advantage into tempo across the map.
tips: Trade on Apprehend cooldown windows.
After level 6, use your lead for map pressure.
Stay disciplined around Ghost timers.

counters.reason: Vayne's mobility and true damage make extended melee trades extremely hard for Darius.
counters.winCondition: Kite tightly, use Tumble reactively, and avoid giving away lane control with bad wave states.
tips: Use Tumble mostly against engage threat.
Pressure only when you have jungle info.
Greedy positioning is how this lane gets thrown.

counters.reason: Malphite stabilizes lane with armor and forces high-value teamfights.
counters.winCondition: You do not need to hard-win lane. Survive, stack armor, and move the game toward teamfight setups.
tips: Do not ego-fight side lane without a reason.
Use your ultimate on real impact windows.
Do not let Fiora take free plates and tempo.

counters.reason: Poppy interrupts dash patterns and forces more controlled trades.

counters.reason: Gragas can neutralize lane flow and deny Fiora easy all-in structure.
counters.winCondition: Keep the lane low-volatility, break up commit timings, and stay useful through utility and setup.

Use wave control to limit Fiora's best windows.

counters.reason: Quinn controls spacing, ranged pressure and map tempo extremely well.

Do not overstay for one extra poke rotation.
Roam with your lead instead of staying top forever.

counters.reason: Camille punishes predictable trade patterns and grows into a stronger side-lane threat.

counters.reason: Jax has strong duel power and can create strong side-lane pressure as the game scales.

counters.reason: Tryndamere can force side-lane pressure and take long trades in favorable windows.

counters.reason: Nasus can survive lane, scale, and later force a very linear side-lane win condition against Gwen if he gets enough time.

Your lane goal is survival first, not domination.

counters.reason: Sett controls frontal trades well and punishes dash commits hard.

Do not get baited into minion-heavy fights.
Your lane plan should be stable, not flashy.

counters.reason: Jax has tools to disrupt engage patterns and remains a powerful duel champion.

counters.reason: Gwen can pressure armor-stacking patterns with sustained AP damage and strong side-lane presence.

counters.reason: Gangplank uses range pressure and cleanse value to make the lane harder to execute cleanly for Mordekaiser.

counters.reason: Range and disengage weaken Renekton's dash-stun pattern significantly.
